jobs.title: Global Head of GBSO
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across five continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

Lonza is seeking a senior leader to head its Global Business Services Organization (GBSO) for Finance, with full accountability for delivering high-quality, standardized, and efficient finance services at enterprise scale. This role owns end-to-end global finance operations across Order-to-Cash, Source-to-Pay, and Record-to-Report, leading a 300+ person organization structured in global process towers.

Operating within a multi-location shared services model across the UK, Costa Rica, and India, the role partners closely with the Lonza Capability Centre to ensure world-class service delivery, scalability, and operational excellence. Reporting to the Head of Corporate Finance, the GBSO acts as the global functional owner for Finance services, driving performance, governance, and continuous improvement across regions.

Beyond core transactional finance, the role leads strategic value creation through global service towers, including Finance Legal Entity Representation, SAR Controlling, and a Finance Centre of Excellence covering SAP access, finance applications, master data governance, and corporate finance project delivery. A key mandate is to modernize finance operations through digitalization and AI, positioning Finance as a high-impact, future-ready global service organization.

Key Responsibilities

• Set and execute the global Finance Shared Services strategy aligned to Lonza’s enterprise goals

• Act as global functional owner for Finance services with clear governance, KPIs, and accountability

• Partner with the Lonza Capability Centre to align service delivery capabilities and infrastructure

• Ensure high‑quality, efficient global delivery across O2C, S2P, R2R, FLER, SAR, and Finance CoE

• Drive global standardization, simplification, and continuous performance improvement

• Lead Finance Shared Services transformation through digitalization, automation, AI, and analytics

• Ensure strong financial governance, controls, and regulatory compliance across all regions

• Oversee Finance Legal Entity Representation and serve as primary audit interface

• Act as a trusted partner to senior Finance, business platforms, and corporate stakeholders

• Lead and develop a high‑performing global leadership team, delivering measurable cost, quality, and value outcomes

Key Requirements

• 15+ years’ experience within shared services, consulting / Finance

• Advanced degree in Finance and/or Business Administration. MBA preferred

• Proven experience with end-to-end accountability for finance process delivery in an operational environment

• Proven leader of large, complex, global organizations operating in matrix environments

• Strong strategic thinker with the ability to translate vision into execution and measurable outcomes

• Demonstrated success leading enterprise‑wide transformation, including process redesign and change management

• Influential leader able to drive alignment and results without direct authority across functions and geographies

• Strong project and change management capability, delivering multiple, complex initiatives in parallel

• Digitally savvy with hands‑on experience leveraging automation, AI, analytics, and modern finance technologies

• Deep finance and business acumen with end‑to‑end ownership of core finance processes (O2C, S2P, R2R)

• Solid understanding of financial governance, controls, compliance, and regulatory requirements

• Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills with senior leadership and business partners

• People‑focused leader committed to talent development, cultural inclusion, accountability, and continuous improvement

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.

People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world — the satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a qualified individual with disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Laboratory Technician QC Microbiology (m/f/d) 60%
jobs.description:
Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten.

Am Standort Visp  wird ein/e Laborant/in der QC Mikrobiologie gesucht. Als Mitglied unseres jungen und dynamischen Mikrobiologie-Teams, bist du unter Einhaltung der GMP- und Sicherheitsrichtlinien verantwortliche für die Routine-Durchführung von verschiedenen mikrobiologischen Methoden. 

Ihre Aufgaben:

• Durchführung analytischer Methoden im Aufgabenbereich, Auswertung, Review und Berichten der Ergebnisse.

• Erstellen von Analysenprotokollen, Prüfanweisungen und Kurzberichten.

• Dokumentation aller Labortätigkeiten entsprechend GMP und internen Richtlinien.

• Einhaltung der Qualitäts- (SOP und GMP) und der Sicherheitsvorschriften.

• Sauberkeit und Ordnung im Labor.

• Methodischer Hauptfokus: Keim Identifizierungen und Endotoxin Messungen.

Ihr Anforderungsprofil:

• Abgeschlossene Ausbildung als Laborant/in, BTA, oder BSC mit Fachrichtung Biologie, Biochemie oder ähnlichem Bereich.

• Fliessend Deutsch und English von Vorteil.

• Hands-on Labor Erfahrung (insbesondere Erfahrung mit mikrobiologischen Analysetechniken) sind ein Plus.

• Kenntnisse im Bereich GMP ist ein Plus .

Lonza’s Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt.

Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

Ihre Aufgaben: • Durchführung analytischer Methoden im Aufgabenbereich, Auswertung, Review und Berichten der Ergebnisse. • Erstellen von Analysenprotokollen, Prüfanweisungen und Kurzberichten. • Dokumentation aller Labortätigkeiten entsprechend GMP und internen Richtlinien. • Einhaltung der Qualitäts- (SOP und GMP) und der Sicherheitsvorschriften. • Sauberkeit und Ordnung im Labor. • Methodischer Hauptfokus: Keim Identifizierungen und Endotoxin Messungen. Ihr Anforderungsprofil: • Abgeschlossene Ausbildung als Laborant/in, BTA, oder BSC mit Fachrichtung Biologie, Biochemie oder ähnlichem Bereich. • Fliessend Deutsch und English von Vorteil. • Hands-on Labor Erfahrung (insbesondere Erfahrung mit mikrobiologischen Analysetechniken) sind ein Plus. • Kenntnisse im Bereich GMP ist ein Plus . Lonza’s Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt. Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten. jobs.title: Anlagemeister Support Infra 80-100% (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Der Anlagemeister – Support Infra gewährleistet durch eine intensive Betreuung seiner ihm zugeteilten Anlagen und Prozesse (Reinstmedien und HVAC) ein nach GMP - Richtlinien funktionsfähiges Produktionsumfeld. Dabei unterstützt er die Projektteams im PCP / PCPSS, welche für die Herstellung von Bioconjugaten verantwortlich sind.
Die Hauptfunktion beinhaltet die Bedienung sowie die korrekte Instandhaltung der Clean-Steam, Purified Water, Water for injection, HeatingVentilation & Air Conditioning Systems HVAC, sowie das Erkennen und Beheben von Störungen der ihm zugeteilten Anlagen und Prozesse.

Was auf Sie wartet:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur.

• Ein integratives und ethisches Arbeitsumfeld.

• Eine Vielzahl von Vorteilen, abhängig von Funktion und Standort.

Ihre Aufgaben:

• Sicherstellen das die Anlagen für Clean-Steam, Purified Water, Water for injection und Heating Ventilation & Air Conditioning Systems (HVAC), sowie alle Energie-Systeme laufend regelkonform den Betrieben PCP und PCPSS zur Verfügung stehen.

• Bearbeitung, Analyse und Behebung von Abweichungen des Umgebungs- und Wassermonitorings sowie dem Monitoring von Prozessgasen im PCP und PCPSS.

• Erstellung und Pflege der notwendigen SOPs für den zugeteilten Bereich

• Unterstützt Fremdfirmen bei Arbeiten in den Betriebsgebäuden und erteilt Arbeitsbewilligungen in seinem zugeteilten Verantwortungsbereich.

• Stellt sicher, dass alle Kühlgeräte und Kühlräume im PCP und PCPSS laufend und regelkonform zur Verfügung stehen

• Vertretung der zugeteilten Bereiche an Behörden- und Kundenaudits

• die Sicherstellung, dass die ihm zugeteilten Systemen/Apparate kontinuierlich und regelkonform betrieben werden.

• die Gewährleistung von funktionsfähigen Systeme/Apparate für die Produktion durch regelmässige Kontrollen und Wartungen.

• die anlagentechnische Schulung/Ausbildung der Operatoren in seinem Aufgabenbereich.

• Sorgt für Sauberkeit und Ordnung in der Anlage und Utilitybereich

• die Unterstützung des AM Supports im Bereich Betriebshygiene PCP

Was wir suchen:

• Abgeschlossene Ausbildung als Chemie- und Pharmatechnologe (Chemikant) oder Laborant Fachrichtung Chemie/Biologie oder vergleichbare Ausbildung

• Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im Bereich GMP Produktion oder Reinstmedien oder im Utility Bereich

• Idealerweise cGMP-Kenntnisse sowie Fachkenntnisse in der Biopharma-Produktion

• Innovativ und ausgeprägtes Organisationstalent

• Hohe Flexibilität, ausgeprägte Teamfähigkeit und hohe Einsatzbereitschaft

Wir bieten attraktive Benefits sowie ein umfassendes Relocation-Paket für talentierte neue Mitarbeitende.

Über Lonza
Bei Lonza sind unsere Mitarbeiter unsere grösste Stärke. An über 30 Standorten auf fünf Kontinenten arbeiten unsere global vernetzten Teams täglich zusammen, um die Medikamente von morgen herzustellen. Unsere Grundwerte: Zusammenarbeit, Verantwortlichkeit, Exzellenz, Leidenschaft und Integrität spiegeln wider, wer wir sind und wie wir zusammenarbeiten. Die Ideen jedes Einzelnen, ob gross oder klein, haben das Potenzial, Millionen von Leben zu verbessern, und genau daran möchten wir Sie teilhaben lassen.

Innovation gedeiht, wenn Menschen unterschiedlichster Herkunft ihre einzigartigen Perspektiven einbringen. Bei Lonza schätzen wir Vielfalt und setzen uns für ein integratives Umfeld für alle Mitarbeiter ein. Wenn Sie bereit sind, die bahnbrechenden Ideen unserer Kunden in praktikable Therapien umzusetzen, freuen wir uns, Sie an Bord begrüssen zu dürfen.

Bereit die Zukunft der Life Sciences zu gestalten? Jetzt bewerben

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: (Senior-) Program Manager, Advanced Synthesis 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across five continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of

The Visp site in Switzerland is growing rapidly, driven by our Advanced Synthesis Business Platform, which includes Bioconjugates and Small Molecules. Our teams specialize in developing and manufacturing drug substances under GMP, helping bring innovative therapies to patients worldwide.

We are hiring (Senior) Program Managers to join our Advanced Synthesis organization. In this role, you’ll be the main link between Lonza and our customers, leading cross-functional teams through the full project lifecycle—from contract to GMP manufacturing and delivery.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. The full list of our local tailored benefits can also be found below:

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

What you will do:

• Lead and deliver customer programs across their full lifecycle—from technology transfer to GMP manufacturing and project closure—ensuring quality, timeliness, and cost-effectiveness.

• Act as a trusted partner and main point of contact for customers, fostering transparent communication and long-term collaboration.

• Inspire and coordinate diverse, cross-functional teams in a matrix environment, promoting shared ownership and high performance.

• Drive program success by managing scope, timelines, and financials, while aligning with strategic business goals.

• Collaborate across functions to manage scope changes, support commercial strategies, and contribute to future opportunities.

• Identify and address risks proactively, ensuring smooth issue resolution and continuous improvement.

• Mentor and support team members, fostering a culture of learning, inclusion, and professional development. 

What we are looking for:

• Degree in Chemistry, Biology, Biotechnology, or a related field; PhD is a plus.

• Experience in project or program management, ideally in pharma or CDMO.

• Strong understanding of technical, commercial, and organizational factors.

• Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and deliver complex programs.

• Knowledge of GMP manufacturing and drug substance development.

• Excellent communication, collaboration, and leadership skills.

• Fluent in English; German is a plus.

About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.description:
Location:  Visp (Switzerland)
Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

This position is based in the quaint and beautiful town of Visp! Ibex Solutions (www.ibex.lonza.com) is a modular complex for the development and production of biological products. It gives companies access to a complete solution, gaining speed and achieving a simplified value chain. The Ibex complex is our contribution to the medicine of tomorrow! Would you like to be a part of this? Start your career at Lonza and apply as a Project Leader Quality Control today!
Communicate with manufacturing, QA, and external customers. Represent QC in cross-functional teams and align strategies with other departments.

What you’ll get:

• An agile career and dynamic working culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

• Along with competitive salaries, you’ll find a wide range of lifestyle, family and leisure benefits available. Depending on your location, these benefits could include family allowances, childcare support, discounts for local businesses and attractions, travel allowances and subsidized meals.

Key responsibilities:

• Establishing and maintaining customer relationships

• Preparing and supporting production campaigns

• Processing change requests and deviations under GMP

• Supporting customer audits and inspections by authorities

• Calculating and monitoring QC costs in the project

• Independent coordination, prioritization and scheduling of tasks

Key requirements:

• PhD or equivalent experience in the Life Sciences (Biology, Biochemistry or Pharmaceutical Sciences)

• Work experience in the pharmaceutical/biotechnological GMP environment and in Quality Control is a strong Plus

• Your assertive, clear and direct communication style allows you to work closely with different collaborators in a professional manner. You enjoy working with many people in various business areas and bring solid organizational and project management skills to handle demanding projects.

• Proficient use of common software applications (MS Office) is required. Knowledge of individual pharmaceutical/laboratory software (e.g. Documentum, LIMS, Trackwise) is an advantage

• Proficiency in English essential, basic German skills needed (B1 level), and motivation to improve rapidly (free German language courses offered)

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.
People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Biotechnologist (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

What you will do:

• Independent operation and execution of upstream related activities according to specified instructions (manufacturing protocols / operating instructions / work instructions / SOP)

• Safe and correct execution of work in compliance with safety regulations, taking into account the cGMP quality requirements

• Responsibility for cleanliness and order at the workplace as well as the assigned rooms and storage areas

• Performing monitoring, maintenance, and cleaning tasks, as well as reporting and rectifying defects and malfunctions in the facility, including their documentation

• Every employee is involved as an equal team member through their active participation in continuous improvement processes

What we are looking for:

• Completed professional training (Apprenticeship)

• Chemical or pharmaceutical-technical training as well as GMP and/or BICO training advantageous

• Good skills in speaking, writing, and comprehension in German or English required.

• Accurate work and documentation practices

• Execution of tasks with a high level of safety and quality awareness

• Willingness to work shifts (4-shift operation) with flexibility in work scheduling

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

German Version:
Der Standort dieser Position ist in Visp, Schweiz. Bei Bedarf steht für berechtigte Kandidaten und ihre Familien Unterstützung beim Umzug zur Verfügung.

Was wir Ihnen bieten:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Ein inklusiver und ethischer Arbeitsplatz

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen

Zusätzlich zu einem wettbewerbsfähigen Gehalt können Sie zahlreiche Vorteile für Lebensstil, Familie und Freizeit erwarten. Die vollständige Liste unserer lokal zugeschnittenen Benefits finden Sie unten.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

Ihre Aufgaben:

• Selbstständige Durchführung und Ausführung von Upstream-bezogenen Tätigkeiten gemäß vorgegebenen Anweisungen (Herstellprotokolle / Arbeitsanweisungen / SOPs)

• Sichere und korrekte Ausführung der Arbeiten unter Einhaltung der Sicherheitsvorschriften sowie unter Berücksichtigung der cGMP-Qualitätsanforderungen

• Verantwortung für Sauberkeit und Ordnung am Arbeitsplatz sowie in den zugewiesenen Räumen und Lagerbereichen

• Durchführung von Überwachungs-, Wartungs- und Reinigungsarbeiten sowie Meldung, Behebung und Dokumentation von Mängeln und Störungen an den Anlagen

• Aktive Mitarbeit als gleichwertiges Teammitglied durch Beteiligung an kontinuierlichen Verbesserungsprozessen

Ihr Profil

• Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung (Lehre)

• Chemische oder pharmazeutisch-technische Ausbildung sowie Kenntnisse in GMP und/oder BICO von Vorteil

• Gute Deutsch- oder Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift erforderlich

• Sorgfältige Arbeits- und Dokumentationsweise

• Durchführung der Aufgaben mit einem hohen Maß an Sicherheits- und Qualitätsbewusstsein

• Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit (4-Schicht-Betrieb) sowie Flexibilität in der Arbeitszeitgestaltung

Bei Lonza sind unsere Mitarbeitenden unsere grösste Stärke. Mit über 30 Standorten auf fünf Kontinenten arbeiten unsere global vernetzten Teams jeden Tag zusammen, um die Medikamente von morgen herzustellen. Unsere Kernwerte – Zusammenarbeit, Verantwortlichkeit, Exzellenz, Leidenschaft und Integrität – spiegeln wider, wer wir sind und wie wir zusammenarbeiten. Jede Idee, ob gross oder klein, hat das Potenzial, Millionen von Leben zu verbessern – und genau an dieser Arbeit möchten wir Sie beteiligen.

Innovation gedeiht, wenn Menschen aus unterschiedlichen Hintergründen ihre einzigartigen Perspektiven einbringen. Bei Lonza schätzen wir Vielfalt und setzen uns für ein inklusives Umfeld für alle Mitarbeitenden ein. Wenn Sie bereit sind, die bahnbrechenden Ideen unserer Kunden in umsetzbare Therapien zu verwandeln, freuen wir uns darauf, Sie an Bord zu begrüssen.

Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences mitzugestalten? Bewerben Sie sich jetzt.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Projektleiter/in Analytik (f/m/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is dedicated individuals working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our individuals own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

Communicate with manufacturing, QA, and customers. Represent QC in project teams. Make decisions independently.

In a matrix organization, you collaborate with labs, program management, production, and quality assurance.

What you'll do:

• Establishing and maintaining customer relationships.

• Preparing and supporting production campaigns.

• Processing change requests and deviations under GMP.

• Supporting customer audits and inspections by authorities.

• Calculating and monitoring QC costs in the project.

• Independent coordination, prioritization and scheduling of tasks.

What we're looking for:

• PhD or equivalent experience in the Life Sciences (Biology, Biochemistry or Pharmaceutical Sciences).

• Experience in the GMP environment and in Quality Control is a plus

• Proficient use of common software applications (MS Office) is required. Knowledge of individual pharmaceutical/laboratory software (e.g., Documentum, LIMS, Trackwise) is a plus.

• You enjoy working with many people in many different business areas and you bring the necessary organizational and project management skills to handle demanding projects. Your communication style is characterized by integrity and assertiveness.

• Excellent communication skills in English is a must, communication skills in German is an advantage.

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.

People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the happiness that comes with improving lives all around the world. The happiness that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a qualified individual with disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically. People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the happiness that comes with improving lives all around the world. The happiness that comes with making a meaningful difference. Lonza is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a qualified individual with disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.description:
Location: the actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

The QA Specialist (QA On The Floor) is responsible to the Associate Director, Head of QA Operations BioAtrium  in joining a strong and innovative team that is perceived as a main contributor to the success of the large scale mammalian operations in our future BioAtrium site.  A great candidate will have strong GMP mindset, have leadership capabilities, envisioning and driving teamwork, quality culture, and innovation with the goal of becoming a role model within Lonza. We are looking for someone comfortable working in a matrix organization, effective with cross-functional projects, someone who can conceptualize where we need to be, how to get there, and drive ideas to completion.  The QA Specialist will within the QA Operations team as QA On The Floor, but will support other QA Ops teams (e.g. Clean Utilities and Environmental Monitoring, QA for QC and Electronic Batch Record Review) They will interact with the the other Operational teams at BioAtrium like Production, Engineering and Project Management. 

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.
Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi  

What you will do:

• Comply with, Develop, implement and monitor quality procedures to ensure compliance with GxP standards, corporate policies, Swissmedic, FDA and other regulatory body regulations and guidelines.

• Support all QA operations activities concerning SOP landscape, electronic batch records, training,  deviation- / CAPA- and change control management

• Support all audits and inspections

• Identify and implement Lean / OPEX approaches in Quality and foster improvement projects

• Provide KPI reporting and execute action plans to address opportunities for improvement

• Work collaboratively with QA Operations and QA department colleagues

What we are looking for:

• Academic degree in a Life Science discipline/ Engineering/ Quality Management or related field

• Experience in working on a regulated Medical device, Drug product or Drug Substance site

• Experience with Lean / Operational Excellence / Six Sigma

• GMP compliance expertise

• Ability to interface and communicate directly with customers and regulatory bodies

• Excellent written and oral communication to include accurate and legible documentation

• Ability to work on call and/or out of normal working hours on site

• Fluency in English is required, German language skills are an advantage

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Paralegal 80-100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
This role provides essential support to the Legal Department on corporate governance, commercial register filings, and administrative excellence. You will act as a key coordinator for all Swiss Lonza entities, ensuring legal compliance and operational efficiency while working closely with legal counsel and international stakeholders.  

  

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.  

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.  

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.  

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.  

• Benefits in Basel: https://bit.ly/3HZNGv2  

  

What you will do:  

• Manage all commercial register updates (entries, changes, and deletions) for Swiss Lonza entities, drafting resolutions and ensuring regulatory compliance.  

• Support the administration of contracts throughout their lifecycle using the CLM tool (e.g. maintaining contract records, updating metadata, tracking deadlines, and coordinating review and signature processes including notarization as needed). 

• Take ownership of the Legal Proceedings Report (LPR) three times a year, ensuring all data is accurate and submitted on schedule.  

• Conduct in-depth research to assist the legal team in navigating complex corporate or commercial legal matters and routine legal inquiries.  

• Act as a liaison between the legal department, international law firms, and notary offices to drive cross-border projects.  

• Oversee the Basel legal team’s shared inbox, handle travel arrangements, process expenses, and manage vendor invoices and payments.  

• Organize global retreats and events while maintaining an efficient archiving system for all critical legal documentation.  

  

What we are looking for:  

• 5–10 years of experience in a Paralegal role, either within an in-house legal department or a private law practice.  

• In-depth knowledge of legal terminology, corporate law principles, and the ability to analyze complex documents for accuracy.  

• Advanced computer skills and experience managing digital signature platforms and financial processing tools.  

• Business fluency in English and German is required; French proficiency is a significant advantage for managing Swiss entity filings.  

• Ideally an Executive Assistant with legal experience, a Paralegal certificate, or a University degree in Law (BLaw/MLaw) or Business.  

• A respectful, patient, and tactful communicator who thrives in a collaborative team and handles confidential information with the utmost integrity.  

• The ability to prioritize a high volume of information under tight schedules while remaining flexible to support the team’s needs.  

  

About Lonza  

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.  

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.  

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below. • Benefits in Basel: https://bit.ly/3HZNGv2 What you will do: • Manage all commercial register updates (entries, changes, and deletions) for Swiss Lonza entities, drafting resolutions and ensuring regulatory compliance. • Support the administration of contracts throughout their lifecycle using the CLM tool (e.g. maintaining contract records, updating metadata, tracking deadlines, and coordinating review and signature processes including notarization as needed). • Take ownership of the Legal Proceedings Report (LPR) three times a year, ensuring all data is accurate and submitted on schedule. • Conduct in-depth research to assist the legal team in navigating complex corporate or commercial legal matters and routine legal inquiries. • Act as a liaison between the legal department, international law firms, and notary offices to drive cross-border projects. • Oversee the Basel legal team’s shared inbox, handle travel arrangements, process expenses, and manage vendor invoices and payments. • Organize global retreats and events while maintaining an efficient archiving system for all critical legal documentation. What we are looking for: • 5–10 years of experience in a Paralegal role, either within an in-house legal department or a private law practice. • In-depth knowledge of legal terminology, corporate law principles, and the ability to analyze complex documents for accuracy. • Advanced computer skills and experience managing digital signature platforms and financial processing tools. • Business fluency in English and German is required; French proficiency is a significant advantage for managing Swiss entity filings. • Ideally an Executive Assistant with legal experience, a Paralegal certificate, or a University degree in Law (BLaw/MLaw) or Business. • A respectful, patient, and tactful communicator who thrives in a collaborative team and handles confidential information with the utmost integrity. • The ability to prioritize a high volume of information under tight schedules while remaining flexible to support the team’s needs. About Lonza At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of. Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board. Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Proposal Support Specialist (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Basel Stücki, Switzerland.

For our successful Drug Product (DP) focused branch located in Basel and Stein, Switzerland, we have exciting opportunities. Become part of a group that enables its customers to meet some of the greatest challenges in patient treatment and join us by applying for the Proposal Support Specialist position. This is a temporary position until end of February 2027.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

What you will do:

• Manage the CPQ database to ensure accurate and timely updates of scope hours and pricing​

• Analyze and extract system data to support SMEs and Department Heads​

• Coordinate with SMEs , Head of Department and Technical Sales for the update and built of BTOs​

• Owner of the annual pricing process., including ​ maintenance of price list used by SMEs, technical sales , PFM, CDs, PMs and Amendment teams.​

• Provide first level IT and process support for colleagues (DP ang globally) to ensure business continuity ​

What we are looking for:

• Bachelor degree in life science preferred or related disciplines

• Very good organizational skills

• Supports a culture of process improvement and problem solving Demonstrates an openness to trying new approaches

• Plans, prioritizes and organizes work to deliver on time

• Willingness to learn and carry out new and complex tasks

• Excellent knowledge of written and spoken English

About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: DeltaV Automation Engineer 80-100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, ensuring a smooth transition to our welcoming community in Visp.

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of. 

We are looking for a DeltaV Automation Engineer or Electrical Instrumentation & Control Engineer (m/f/d) to join us in a dynamic, cutting-edge environment where your work will significantly contribute to shaping the future of medicine.

What you’ll get:

• An agile career and dynamic working culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

• A variety of benefits dependant on role and location

• The full list of our global benefits can be also found on https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits.

What you will do:

• Planning and realization of electrical MSR readiness (maintenance, changeovers, improvements) within the available budget and timelines

• Ensuring seamless operation and flawless performance of high-automated Biologics facilities equipped with DeltaV and MES systems. Equivalent experience is also acceptable for educational requirements like MES

• Managing GMP-compliant activities, including testing, documentation, and change management using systems such as Kneat and Ubik

• Preparing and updating plant documentation in SAP/COMOS to ensure strict compliance with regulatory standards

• Determining and implementing continuous improvements in equipment and maintenance processes to improve efficiency and reliability

• Collaborating with cross-functional teams to refine internal procedures and achieve optimized workflows

What we are looking for:

• Equivalent experience in Electrical or System Engineering or a related field, including University Degree or equivalent experience, or vocational education (EFZ, FH, HF, BSc.)

• Proven experience in the operation and maintenance of pharmaceutical plants or the biotech industry

• Proficiency in SAP, COMOS (Plant Engineering Software), DeltaV (Process Control System), and MES Syncade (Manufacturing Execution System) is highly desirable

• Proficient in English and/or German to competently communicate in our diverse, collaborative environment

• Familiarity with GMP standards and protocols to ensure compliance in a regulated manufacturing setting

• A self-reliant, trustworthy, and effective working approach that ensures successful completion of tasks and projects

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.

People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

We are looking for a DeltaV Automation Engineer or Electrical Instrumentation & Control Engineer (m/f/d) to join us in a dynamic, cutting-edge environment where your work will significantly contribute to shaping the future of medicine. What you’ll get: • An agile career and dynamic working culture • An inclusive and ethical workplace • Compensation programs that recognize high performance • A variety of benefits dependant on role and location • The full list of our global benefits can be also found on https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits. What you will do: • Planning and realization of electrical MSR readiness (maintenance, changeovers, improvements) within the available budget and timelines • Ensuring seamless operation and flawless performance of high-automated Biologics facilities equipped with DeltaV and MES systems. Equivalent experience is also acceptable for educational requirements like MES • Managing GMP-compliant activities, including testing, documentation, and change management using systems such as Kneat and Ubik • Preparing and updating plant documentation in SAP/COMOS to ensure strict compliance with regulatory standards • Determining and implementing continuous improvements in equipment and maintenance processes to improve efficiency and reliability • Collaborating with cross-functional teams to refine internal procedures and achieve optimized workflows What we are looking for: • Equivalent experience in Electrical or System Engineering or a related field, including University Degree or equivalent experience, or vocational education (EFZ, FH, HF, BSc.) • Proven experience in the operation and maintenance of pharmaceutical plants or the biotech industry • Proficiency in SAP, COMOS (Plant Engineering Software), DeltaV (Process Control System), and MES Syncade (Manufacturing Execution System) is highly desirable • Proficient in English and/or German to competently communicate in our diverse, collaborative environment • Familiarity with GMP standards and protocols to ensure compliance in a regulated manufacturing setting • A self-reliant, trustworthy, and effective working approach that ensures successful completion of tasks and projects Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically. People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Operator Utilities / Mitarbeiter technischer Unterhalt 80-100% (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Umzugsunterstützung steht berechtigen Kandidaten und ihren Familien bei Bedarf zur Verfügung.

Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten.

Ibex® Solutions ist ein modularer Komplex für die Entwicklung und Herstellung von biologischen Produkten. Unternehmen können damit auf eine Komplettlösung zugreifen, dabei an Schnelligkeit gewinnen und eine vereinfachte Wertschöpfungskette erreichen.

Als Operator Utilities / Mitarbeiter Technischer Unterhalt (m/w/d) im Schichtbetrieb in Visp bist du für das Bedienen und Überwachen komplexer Bio-Produktionsanlagen verantwortlich. Gemeinsam mit deinen Kolleginnen und Kollegen behebst du Störungen, führst Reinigungs- und Wartungsarbeiten durch, arbeitest unter anderem in Reinräumen und dokumentierst die Produktionsschritte. Auch in hektischen Zeiten behältst du den Überblick und schätzt den engen Austausch mit verschiedenen Funktionen (Biotechnolog:innen, Betriebsingenieur:innen, QA etc.).

Was wir dir bieten:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Einen integrativen und ethischen Arbeitsplatz

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen

• Neben einem wettbewerbsfähigen Gehalt erwarten dich zahlreiche Vorteile in den Bereichen Lifestyle, Familie und Freizeit. Die vollständige Liste unserer globalen Leistungen findest du auch unter: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

Deine Aufgaben:

• Betrieb der Utilities (Clean Utilities, Black Utilities, HVAC und Abwassersysteme)

• Erkennen, Interpretieren und Beheben von Störungen und Abweichungen

• Abgleich betriebsübergreifender Prozessschritte (Zusammenhänge, Abhängigkeiten, Abläufe)

• Koordination paralleler Tätigkeiten

• Erkennen und Setzen der richtigen Prioritäten

Dein Profil:

• Abgeschlossene Berufslehre (EFZ) oder Technikerschule (HF) in der Fachrichtung Pharma/Chemie oder in der Sanitär-/Heizungsbranche

• Handwerkliches Geschick und technisches Verständnis

• Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift; Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil

• Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit (4-Schichtbetrieb) sowie Flexibilität in der Arbeitsplanung

Lonzas Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt.

Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen.

Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences mitzugestalten? Dann bewirb dich jetzt!

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Trainee Logistics - (temporary for 2 years) - (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland.

The Trainee will be part and responsible to support the logistics operational excellence team in Visp and it’s project leads within the execution of EO improvement/efficiency projects. The trainee will support from the definition phase to the improvement / implementation phase to optimize E2E Logistics processes at the visp site.

The role of this person is to support and maintain structures (e.g. organization, decision, communication, documentation flows, in accordance with project management, in order to respect project management processes and increase project team efficiency.

The hired candidate will be responsible for specific project tasks. These tasks will include developing metrics, tracking progress, prepare and orchestrate workshops and reporting to management. The purpose is to assist project leads and the logistics teams.

They will also act as an active support for preparing regular reviews of the ongoing projects and highlight current issues/gaps.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OBoAVhfTkTQ

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture. An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

• In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. 

​Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

What you will do:

• Responsible for internal coordination and support of project tasks in order to balance the effort of the overall Logistics and Supply Chain team.

• Participate and support in coordinating, issuing project related general and specific documents, reports and presentations.

• Co-ordination and follow up with cross-functional departments (Logistics, Plant Logistics, Supply Chain), tracking activities and deliverables (like list of deliverables, installation list and timely issue of deliverables).

• Responsible for the development of PowerBI dashboards.

• Organize internal workshops and support team members to establish agendas, meeting minutes, actions lists etc.

• Support daily management activities by providing assistance in monitoring and tracking important metrics on a daily and monthly basis. Aid in the implementation and tracking of planned activities.

What we are looking for:

• Degree or ongoing studies in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or a related field (B.Sc., M.Sc., Dipl. Ing., or equivalent).

• Initial knowledge or strong interest in chemical and/or pharmaceutical supply chains is a plus.

• Curiosity and openness to work across different disciplines.

• Motivated and proactive team player with a willingness to learn and contribute.

• Good knowledge of common IT tools; experience with SharePoint, Power BI, and Excel is an advantage.

• Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a team environment.

• Flexible mindset with the ability to adapt to changing priorities, timelines, and tasks.

• Basic analytical and problem‑solving skills with a desire to develop them further.

• Clear and confident written and verbal communication skills.

• Willingness to support administrative and organizational tasks as part of the learning role.

• Reliable and persistent in following up with partners to collect required information.

• Interest in process improvement methodologies; Lean Six Sigma knowledge or certification is a plus.

• Good command of English and German.

• First exposure to a cGMP environment is beneficial but not required.

About Lonza
 
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
 
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
 
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Elektroinstallateur EFZ / Automatiker EFZ - EMR-Schicht 80-100 %(m/w/d)

titleByLocale.it: Installatore/trice elettrico/a CFC / Automatiker CFC - EMR-Schicht 80-100 %(m/w/d)

jobs.description:
Umzugsunterstützung steht berechtigen Kandidaten und ihren Familien bei Bedarf zur Verfügung. 

Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten.

Am Standort Visp  wird ein Elektroinstallateur EFZ / Automatiker EFZ (m/w/d) für die EMR-Schicht gesucht.

Was wir dir bieten:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Einen integrativen und ethischen Arbeitsplatz

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen

• Neben einem wettbewerbsfähigen Gehalt erwarten dich zahlreiche Vorteile in den Bereichen Lifestyle, Familie und Freizeit. Die vollständige Liste unserer globalen Leistungen findest du auch unter: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

Deine Aufgaben:

• Störungsbehebung im 4-Schicht-Betrieb von EMR-Anlagen (Elektro-, Mess- und Regeltechnik)

• Präventive Instandhaltung von EMR-Anlagen

• Reparaturen und Kontrollen an Elektrogeräten

• Dokumentation nach GxP Richtlinien (Datenintegrität)

• Kalibrierung, Kontrolle und Handhabung von Messmitteln

Dein Profil:

• Abgeschlossene Ausbildung zum Automatiker EFZ, Elektroinstallateur EFZ, Elektroniker EFZ oder einem artverwandten Beruf

• Grundkenntnisse Mess- und Regeltechnik sind von Vorteil

• Bereitschaft zu kontinuierlicher Weiterbildung

• Freude an abteilungsübergreifender Arbeit sowie selbstständiges und flexibles Arbeiten im Team

• Zuverlässigkeit, Belastbarkeit sowie unternehmerisches Handeln und offene, direkte Kommunikation

• Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil

Lonza’s Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt.

Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen.

Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences mitzugestalten? Dann bewirb dich jetzt!

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Elektroinstallateur EFZ 80-100% (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Umzugsunterstützung steht berechtigen Kandidaten und ihren Familien bei Bedarf zur Verfügung. 

Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten.

Für unseren Standort in Visp suchen wir einen Elektroinstallateur EFZ.

Was wir dir bieten:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Einen integrativen und ethischen Arbeitsplatz

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen

• Neben einem wettbewerbsfähigen Gehalt erwarten dich zahlreiche Vorteile in den Bereichen Lifestyle, Familie und Freizeit. Die vollständige Liste unserer globalen Leistungen findest du auch unter: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

Deine Aufgaben: 

• Durchführung von Installations-, und Inbetriebsetzungsarbeiten von EMR Anlagen (Elektro-, Mess- und Regeltechnik)

• Präventive Instandhaltung und Störungsbehebung von EMR Anlagen

• Messungen und Sicherheitskontrollen nach CH-Normen

• Dokumentation nach GxP Richtlinien (Datenintegrität)

• Betreuung und Unterstützung von Lehrlingen

Dein Profil:

• Abgeschlossene Ausbildung zum Elektroinstallateur EFZ

• Grundkenntnisse Mess- und Regeltechnik sind von Vorteil

• Bereitschaft zu kontinuierlicher Weiterbildung

• Freude an abteilungsübergreifender Arbeit sowie selbstständiges und flexibles Arbeiten im Team

• Zuverlässigkeit, Belastbarkeit sowie unternehmerisches Handeln und offene, direkte Kommunikation

• Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil

Lonzas Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt.

Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen.

Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences mitzugestalten? Dann bewirb dich jetzt!

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Servicetechniker Reinraum 80-100% (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Umzugsunterstützung steht berechtigen Kandidaten und ihren Familien bei Bedarf zur Verfügung.

Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten.

Für unseren Standort in Visp suchen wir ein Mitarbeiter für das Team Automatik (Reinraummessungen).

Was wir dir bieten:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Einen integrativen und ethischen Arbeitsplatz

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen

• Neben einem wettbewerbsfähigen Gehalt erwarten dich zahlreiche Vorteile in den Bereichen Lifestyle, Familie und Freizeit. Die vollständige Liste unserer globalen Leistungen findest du auch unter: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

Deine Aufgaben:

• Durchführen von Reinraummessungen an Chemie-, Pharma- und Bio-Anlagen

• Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Sicherheits- und Qualitätsstandards während aller Tätigkeiten

• Rezertifizierung der Reinräume

• Dokumentation nach GxP Richtlinien (Datenintegrität)

Dein Profil:

• Abgeschlossene Ausbildung zum Automatiker EFZ, Elektroinstallateur EFZ, Elektroniker EFZ oder einem technischen Beruf

• Bereitschaft zu kontinuierlicher Weiterbildung

• Freude an abteilungsübergreifender Arbeit sowie selbstständiges und flexibles Arbeiten im Team

• Zuverlässigkeit, Belastbarkeit sowie unternehmerisches Handeln und offene, direkte Kommunikation

• Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil

Lonzas Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt.

Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen.

Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences mitzugestalten? Dann bewirb dich jetzt!

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

• Selbstständiger Unterhalt und  Störungsbehebungsarbeiten von Waagen

• Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Sicherheits- und Qualitätsstandards während aller Tätigkeiten

• Dokumentation nach GxP Richtlinien (Datenintegrität)

• Neuinstallation und Inbetriebsetzung von Waagensystemen

Dein Profil 

• Abgeschlossene Ausbildung zum Automatiker EFZ, Elektroinstallateur EFZ, Elektroniker EFZ oder einem technischen Beruf

• Bereitschaft zu kontinuierlicher Weiterbildung

• Freude an abteilungsübergreifender Arbeit sowie selbstständiges und flexibles Arbeiten im Team

• Zuverlässigkeit, Belastbarkeit sowie unternehmerisches Handeln und offene, direkte Kommunikation

• Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil

Lonzas Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. jobs.title: Quality Assurance Expert Analytics, Drug Product Services - 12 months fix-term contract 80-100%

titleByLocale.it: Quality Assurance Expert Analytics, Drug Product Services - 12 months fix-term contract 80-100%
titleByLocale.en: Quality Assurance Expert Analytics, Drug Product Services - 12 months fix-term contract 80-100%
titleByLocale.de: Qualität Assurance Expert Analytics, Drug Product Services - 12 months fix-term contract 80-100%
titleByLocale.fr: Qualité Assurance Expert Analytics, Drug Product Services - 12 months fix-term contract 80-100%

jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Basel Stücki, Switzerland.

For our successful Drug Product Services (DPS) focused branch located in Basel and Stein, Switzerland, we have exciting opportunities. Become part of a group that enables its customers to meet some of the greatest challenges in patient treatment and join us by applying for the QA Expert Analytics DPS position.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

What you will do:

• Establish and maintain Quality Management System for Drug Product Services

• Ensure proper qualification of Drug Product Services staff and adherence to GMP for stability studies and reference standards

• Review and approve analytical methods, qualification/validation/transfer protocols and reports as well as analytic related SOPs

• Supervise and execute GMP compliance activities and act as last approver of deviations for Analytics/Quality Control and OOS investigations

• Act as first contact concerning compliance issues for local and shared systems (e.g. LIMS) including approval of specifications in LIMS

• Counsel different groups on projects and GMP aspects

What we are looking for:

• Academic degree (Master or higher) in Chemistry, Biochemistry, Pharmaceutical Sciences or related field

• Minimum 5-6 years working experience in Quality Assurance in pharmaceutical industries and cGMP controlled environment, preferable in QC.

• Good working knowledge in EU and US GMP regulations relevant for audit/inspections, qualification/validation, compliance systems

• Sound knowledge in testing of biological drug products

• Excellent communication, decision making skills and ability to provide solutions for complex challenges

• Ability to work independently and a team player, being detailed oriented, precise, pro-active and well organized

• Excellent knowledge of written and spoken English

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Operator Visual Inspection 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Join our brand new Fill‑Finish facility in Stein as an Operator Visual Inspection, performing manual inspections today while supporting the transition to semi‑ and fully automated processes. This includes qualification runs and routine inspections in a controlled darkroom setting. We’re looking for a motivated and resilient individual who thrives in structured environments, follows GMP guidelines, and contributes to both the project phase and routine production excellence. Check out Your Career in Drug Product  Services at Lonza!

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk 

What you will do:

• Manual, semi-automated, and fully automated visual inspection of liquid and lyophilized vials and syringes in a cleanroom setting

• Classification of defects in accordance with regulations

• Regular qualification and requalification using existing test sets

• Inspection of test runs / threshold studies

• Support in deviations and root cause analysis

What we are looking for:

• Strong attention to detail and cleanliness, with an uncompromising approach to quality and safety

• Willing to work in a cleanroom

• Experience in a GMP production environment is highly desirable

• Experience in visual inspection, including relevant further training is a plus

• Completed vocational training (e.g. as a chemical or pharmaceutical technologist) is an advantage

• Fluency in German is a must and basic knowledge of English

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
